Laying there on the hospital bed, eyes were narrow, blue and red
You took a draw of breath and said to me

took.linePrev: We're gonna put your heart back together
took.line: So all those little pieces they took from you
took.lineNext: They're coming back now
stanza: I ran into Perry at Lowell's place
Her gemstone eyes caught my gaze
She said, "Oh, dear, I can see you've had a rough few months
But this year, it's gonna be a good one
I'll count to three and tap your shoulder
We're gonna put your heart back together
So all those little pieces they took from you
They're coming back now
They'll miss 'em too
So close your eyes
I'll count to three
Take a deep breath
Count with me"

took.linePrev: All the glory when He took our place
took.line: But He took my shoulders and He shook my face
took.lineNext: And He takes and He takes and He takes
stanza: In the morning when you finally go
And the nurse runs in with her head hung low
And the cardinal hits the window
In the morning in the winter shade
On the first of March, on the holiday
I thought I saw you breathing
All the glory that the Lord has made
And the complications when I see His face
In the morning in the window
All the glory when He took our place
But He took my shoulders and He shook my face
And He takes and He takes and He takes

took.linePrev: Took us all the way to New Orleans
took.line: I took my harpoon out of my dirty red bandanna
took.lineNext: And was blowin' sad while Bobby sang the blues
stanza: I took my harpoon out of my dirty red bandanna
And was blowin' sad while Bobby sang the blues
With them windshield wipers slappin' time and
Bobby clappin' hands we finally sang up every song
That driver knew

took.linePrev: He went to work when he'd spent his last dime
took.line: And Sammy took to stealing when he got that empty feeling
took.lineNext: For a hundred dollar habit without overtime
stanza: Sam Stone's welcome home didn't last too long
He went to work when he'd spent his last dime
And Sammy took to stealing when he got that empty feeling
For a hundred dollar habit without overtime
And the gold rolled through his veins like a thousand railroad trains
And eased his mind in the hours that he chose
While the kids ran around wearin' other people's clothes

took.line: Drivin' through the road work, oh, the work they took forever on
took.lineNext: The road cones blur like mem'ries of the miles we shared between
stanza: Drivin' through the road work, oh, the work they took forever on
The road cones blur like mem'ries of the miles we shared between
The place you learned to say your prayers, the place I took to prayin'
Loadin' in and breakin' down my road dog door deal dreams
Long before we ever met, I made up my direction
Long before I knew the half of half I'm sure of now
Though I'd say it ain't the way that you'd have gone about it
You follow me and lead me on and never let me down

took.linePrev: He'll wrap you up in his arms, tell you that you've been good girls and boys
took.line: He'll rekindle all of those dreams it took you a lifetime to destroy
took.lineNext: He'll reach deep into the hole, heal your shrinking soul
stanza: He'll wrap you up in his arms, tell you that you've been good girls and boys
He'll rekindle all of those dreams it took you a lifetime to destroy
He'll reach deep into the hole, heal your shrinking soul
Mm, but people, you know you're never coming back
He's a ghost, he's a god, he's a man, he's a guru
We're whispering his name through this disappearing land
But hidden in his coat is a red right hand

took.line: The place you learned to say your prayers, the place I took to prayin'

The road cones blur like mem'ries of the miles we shared between

Loadin' in and breakin' down my road dog door deal dreams
Long before we ever met, I made up my direction
Long before I knew the half of half I'm sure of now
Though I'd say it ain't the way that you'd have gone about it
You follow me and lead me on and never let me down

took.linePrev: I was gambling in Havana
took.line: I took a little risk
took.lineNext: Send lawyers, guns, and money
stanza: I was gambling in Havana
I took a little risk
Send lawyers, guns, and money
Dad, get me out of this
Ha!

took.linePrev: The hawks against the doves
took.line: I took a job in the steamie
took.lineNext: Down on Cauldrum Street
stanza: I was nineteen when I came to town
They called it the Summer of Love
They were burning babies, burning flags
The hawks against the doves
I took a job in the steamie
Down on Cauldrum Street
And I fell in love with a laundry girl
Who was working next to me

took.linePrev: And caught the Sunday smell of someone fryin' chicken
took.line: And it took me back to somethin'
took.lineNext: That I'd lost somehow, somewhere along the way
stanza: I'd smoked my brain the night before
On cigarettes and songs that I'd been pickin'
But I lit my first and watched a small kid
Cussin' at  a can that he was kickin'
Then I crossed the empty street
And caught the Sunday smell of someone fryin' chicken
And it took me back to somethin'
That I'd lost somehow, somewhere along the way

took.line: You took my sadness out of context
took.lineNext: At the Mariners Apartment Complex
stanza: You took my sadness out of context
At the Mariners Apartment Complex
I ain't no candle in the wind
I'm the board, the lightning, the thunder
Kind of girl who's gonna make you wonder
Who you are and who you've been

took.songTitle: I Get a Kick Out of You
took.linePrev: Some get a kick from cocaine
took.line: I'm sure that if I took even one sniff
took.lineNext: That would bore me terrifically too
stanza: I get no kick from champagne
Mere alcohol doesn't thrill me at all
So tell me why should it be true
That I get a kick out of you
Some get a kick from cocaine
I'm sure that if I took even one sniff
That would bore me terrifically too
Yet I get a kick out of you

took.linePrev: Running far, running fast to the dead
took.line: He took off all their clothes for them
took.lineNext: He put a cloth on their lips
stanza: He dressed up like a clown for them
With his face paint white and red
And on his best behavior
In a dark room on the bed
He kissed them all
He'd kill ten thousand people
With a sleight of his hand
Running far, running fast to the dead
He took off all their clothes for them
He put a cloth on their lips
Quiet hands, quiet kiss on the mouth

took.linePrev: There was a ragged band that followed in our footsteps
took.line: Running before time took our dreams away
took.lineNext: Leaving the myriad small creatures trying to tie us to the ground
stanza: Beyond the horizon of the place we lived when we were young
In a world of magnets and miracles
Our thoughts strayed constantly and without boundary
The ringing of the division bell had begun
Along the Long Road and on down the Causeway
Do they still meet there by the Cut
There was a ragged band that followed in our footsteps
Running before time took our dreams away
Leaving the myriad small creatures trying to tie us to the ground
To a life consumed by slow decay

took.linePrev: Michigan seems like a dream to me now
took.line: It took me four days to hitchhike from Saginaw
took.lineNext: I've come to look for America
stanza: Kathy, I said, as we boarded a Greyhound in Pittsburgh
Michigan seems like a dream to me now
It took me four days to hitchhike from Saginaw

took.linePrev: But something kept me standing by that hospital bed
took.line: I should have quit, but instead I took care of you
took.lineNext: You made me sleep and uneven, and I didn't believe them
stanza: But something kept me standing by that hospital bed
I should have quit, but instead I took care of you
You made me sleep and uneven, and I didn't believe them
When they told me that there was no saving you

took.linePrev: Well, your enemy is sleeping and his woman is free
took.line: Yes, and thanks for the trouble you took from her eyes
took.lineNext: I thought it was there for good, so I never tried
stanza: Yes, and thanks for the trouble you took from her eyes
I thought it was there for good, so I never tried
And Jane came by with a lock of your hair
She said that you gave it to her
That night that you planned to go clear
Sincerely, L. Cohen

took.linePrev: Lonesome
took.line: I took some comfort there
took.lineNext: Then I'm laying out my winter clothes
stanza: Asking only workman's wages
I come looking for a job
But I get no offers
Just a come-on from the whores
On Seventh Avenue
I do declare
There were times when I was so
Lonesome
I took some comfort there

took.linePrev: I nearly put a bullet in my brain
took.line: When the rhythm took me in
took.lineNext: I am a master hunter
stanza: I don't stare at water anymore
Water doesn't do what it did before
Took me in and to the edge of insane
When I only meant to swim
I nearly put a bullet in my brain
When the rhythm took me in

took.linePrev: Storyline fever got its hooks in you
took.line: You took your love to the marketplace
took.lineNext: In the wanton corner of a Western state
stanza: You took your love to the marketplace
In the wanton corner of a Western state
Where every other shopper is a basket case
Just realizing the horizon forms a frowning face
Hanging around those haunts at night
Where half the clientele was part poltergeist
Grinding an axe against all that exists
Minus their protected exceptions to it

took.linePrev: Because her hair was black and her eyes were blue
took.line: I took her hand and I give her a twirl
took.lineNext: And I lost my heart to a Galway girl
stanza: And I ask you, friend, what's a fella to do?
Because her hair was black and her eyes were blue
I took her hand and I give her a twirl
And I lost my heart to a Galway girl

took.linePrev: Something I want to say (Lover, lover, lover)
took.line: When you left, you took all of me with you
took.lineNext: My Lord, I wonder, do you want to hear me scream, plead and plead, "Please"? (E…
stanza: Oh baby, don't go
Please, come back, baby
Something I want to say (Lover, lover, lover)
When you left, you took all of me with you
My Lord, I wonder, do you want to hear me scream, plead and plead, "Please"? (E…
Oh please, baby (Every moment)
Come back home, little girl (Every moment)
Oh, baby (Every moment)
Please

took.linePrev: A toothless hound-dog choking on a feather
took.line: But I took my fishing pole (fearing your fever)
took.lineNext: Down to the swimming hole, where there grows a bitter herb
stanza: But I took my fishing pole (fearing your fever)
Down to the swimming hole, where there grows a bitter herb
That blooms but one day a year, by the riverside —
I’d bring it here:

took.linePrev: He really wasn't where it's at
took.line: After he took from you everything he could steal?
took.lineNext: How does it feel?
stanza: Aw, you never turned around to see the frowns
On the jugglers and the clowns when they all did tricks for you
Never understood that it ain't no good
You shouldn't let other people get your kicks for you
You used to ride on a chrome horse with your diplomat
Who carried on his shoulder a Siamese cat
Ain't it hard when you discover that
He really wasn't where it's at
After he took from you everything he could steal?

took.linePrev: My sleeping heart woke, and my waking heart spoke
took.line: Then there was a silence you took to mean something:
took.lineNext: Mean, Run, sing
stanza: Then there was a silence you took to mean something:
Mean, Run, sing
For alive you will evermore be
And the plague of the greasy black engines a-skulking
Has gone east
While you’re left to explain them to me —
Released
From their hairless and blind cavalry

triumph.linePrev: Shine on (Shine on), you crazy diamond (You crazy diamond)
triumph.line: And we'll bask in the shadow of yesterday's triumph
triumph.lineNext: And sail on the steel breeze
stanza: And we'll bask in the shadow of yesterday's triumph
And sail on the steel breeze
Come on, you boy child, you winner and loser
Come on, you miner for truth and delusion, and shine

partner.line: Come on, you miner for truth and delusion, and shine

triumph.linePrev: Everything that you love always goes out of style
triumph.line: Took a good chance on a triumph or two
triumph.lineNext: Hangin' around town never looked good on you
stanza: Moving your lips like you're wanting to smile
Everything that you love always goes out of style
Took a good chance on a triumph or two
Hangin' around town never looked good on you

toward.linePrev: Or just how wrong you are
toward.line: My feet are always heavy as I inch toward the door
toward.lineNext: I thought we'd leave us for ourselves a hundred times before
stanza: My feet are always heavy as I inch toward the door
I thought we'd leave us for ourselves a hundred times before
But I guess we're always leaving, even when we look the same
And it eases me somehow to know that even this will change

toward.songTitle: The Days of Wine and Roses
toward.linePrev: Smile and run away like a child at play
toward.line: Through a meadow land toward a closing door
toward.lineNext: A door marked 'Nevermore' that wasn't there before
stanza: 2 ContributorsThe Days of Wine and Roses LyricsThe days of wine and roses
Smile and run away like a child at play
Through a meadow land toward a closing door
A door marked 'Nevermore' that wasn't there before

toward.linePrev: I knew these birds were omens but of what I wasn't sure
toward.line: They were flying out toward the island where we hoped to move
stanza: 37 ContributorsRavens Lyrics“Ravens” is the third song from A Crow Looked at Me…
I'd just finished splitting up the scrap two-by-fours into kindling
I glanced up at the half moon, pink, chill refinery cloud light
Two big black birds flew over, their wings whooshing and low
Two ravens
(But only two)
Their black feathers tinted in the sunset
I knew these birds were omens but of what I wasn't sure
They were flying out toward the island where we hoped to move
You were probably inside
You were probably aching, wanting not to die
Your body transformed
I couldn't bear to look so I turned my head west
Like an early death
Now I can only see you on the fridge in lifeless pictures
And in every dream I have at night
And in every room I walk into like
Here, where I sit the next October, still seeing your eyes, pleading and afraid
Full of love
Calling out from another place
Because you're not here
I watched you die in this room, then I gave your clothes away
I'm sorry, I had to
And now I'll move
I will move with our daughter
We will ride over water
With your ghost underneath the boat
What was you is now burnt bones
And I cannot be at home
I'm running, grief flailing
I pray for her who prays for me
A safe return to my belle France
We prayed these wars would end all wars
In war, we know, is no romance
And I pray our child will never see
A little Corporal again
Point toward a foreign shore
Captivate the hearts of men

toward.linePrev: They make this awful sizzling sound
toward.line: I cast my gaze toward the pavement
toward.lineNext: Too many blood stains on the ground
stanza: When the sun goes down, the armies of the voiceless
Several hundred-thousand strong
Come out without their bandages
Their voices raised in song
And when the street lights sputter out
They make this awful sizzling sound
I cast my gaze toward the pavement
Too many blood stains on the ground

toward.lineNext: Though you may find, from time to time
stanza: Just move on up
Toward your destination
Though you may find, from time to time
Complication

title.lineNext: I only hope somebody requests it

You wrote about me on every new record
And I'll show up in a title of your song
I only hope somebody requests it

Have me divorced by the time of the chorus
There's no need to change any sentence

And he can't remember where he parked his car
Or to whom he lost the keys
But he's full of angst and hillbilly haiku
What's a poor Ft. Worth boy to do?

(waving the flag
Feeling it drag)
Like a bump on a bump on a log, baby;
Like I'm in a fistfight with the fog, baby;
Step, ball-change, and a pirouette!

I say

trout.line: For a spell, as naked as a trout
trout.lineNext: The wandering eye that I have caught
stanza: Well, you'd be shocked at the state of things
The whole place had just cleared right out
It was hotter than hell, so I laid me by a spring
For a spell, as naked as a trout

trout.linePrev: The buffalo's gone, and the mountain top's flat
trout.line: The trout in the streams are all hermaphrodites
trout.lineNext: You lean to the left but you vote to the right
stanza: The temple's in ruins, the bankers get fat
The buffalo's gone, and the mountain top's flat
The trout in the streams are all hermaphrodites
You lean to the left but you vote to the right
And it feels like déjà vu, the Sun goes down and I'm still missing you
Counting the cost of love that got lost
And under my Gulf Stream, in circular pools
There's ninety-nine cents worth of drunkards and fools

trout.linePrev: Ten feet of water at the bottom of the lake
trout.line: I imagined trout bumping against me in the low diminished light
trout.lineNext: Holding my breath trying to be a boulder
stanza: At night I sit and picture myself curled up beneath
Ten feet of water at the bottom of the lake
I imagined trout bumping against me in the low diminished light
Holding my breath trying to be a boulder
Eroding, to join you in re-mingling with a background
Of churned muck coalescing in the dark
But to get ground back down to matter only
Eternal and dumb becoming not a thing
Abdicating form

task.line: Thou thy worldly task hast done
task.lineNext: Home art gone, and ta'en thy wages
stanza: Fear no more the heat o' th' sun

Thou thy worldly task hast done
Home art gone, and ta'en thy wages

task.line: And the task was the hardest thing she'd ever do

tribute.line: The tributes we have left to rust in the park
tribute.lineNext: Swearing that our hair stood on end
stanza: So we all raise a standard
To which the wise and honest soul may repair
To which a hunter
A hundred years from now, may look and despair
And see with wonder

For the Western Front
Where work might count

(and what lies under, now)

Too soon—

It's turtles all the way down the line

To and through the myth that we all call space and time

tribe.linePrev: Many years ago along the Illinois-Wisconsin border
tribe.lineNext: They found two babies in the woods, white babies
stanza: Many years ago along the Illinois-Wisconsin border
There was this Indian tribe
They found two babies in the woods, white babies
One of them was named Elizabeth
She was the fairer of the two
While the smaller and more fragile one was named Marie
Having never seen white girls before
And living on the two lakes known as the Twin Lakes
They named the larger and more beautiful lake, Lake Elizabeth
And thus the smaller lake that was hidden from the highway
Became known forever as Lake Marie

tribe.linePrev: Why aye man, why aye, why aye man
tribe.line: We're the nomad tribes, travelling boys  (why aye, why aye man)
tribe.lineNext: In the dust and dirt and the racket and the noise (why aye, why aye man)
stanza: We're the nomad tribes, travelling boys  (why aye, why aye man)
In the dust and dirt and the racket and the noise (why aye, why aye man)
Drills and hammers, diggers and picks (why aye, why aye man)
Mixing concrete, laying bricks (why aye, why aye man)
There's English, Irish, Scots, the lot
United Nation's what we've got
Brickies, chippies, every trade
German-built and British-made

tis.songTitle: My Old Kentucky Home, Good Night
tis.linePrev: The sun shines bright in the old Kentucky home
tis.lineNext: The corn top's ripe and the meadow's in the bloom
stanza: The sun shines bright in the old Kentucky home
'Tis summer, the darkies are gay
The corn top's ripe and the meadow's in the bloom
While the birds make music all the day
The young folks roll on the little cabin floor
All merry, all happy, and bright
By 'n by hard times comes a-knocking at the door
Then my old Kentucky home, good night
I've got my banjo tuned
But now I've lost the band
I must have missed the turn
I never learn it seems
My music is my dreams
Tis what I do
They close the hall
And put away the microphones
I'm all alone and I'd lay scratching at the strings
Then my banjo sings as banjoes often do
I'm coming through, and loving you

Under your dark glasses
Privately probing the public rooms
And peeking through keyholes in numbered doors
